speaker: MATT
raw_text: Seven points of acid, got it. As it slams, it begins to burn the back of the skin. You watch a bit of steam rise off of its shoulder. It definitely is wounding the creature. This large, swollen troll is still making a break. I need everyone to roll initiative now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Essentially, yeah. You guys all spin and watch this happening. Kiri's over by the edge of the tree with you, and Caleb, where were you?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Towards the back of the group, never near the front.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You would have probably been over here, near Nott.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I fired a shot that missed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Magic Missile, level two, four darts, total of 16 force damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: Reflex save-- Dexterity. Dexterity saving throw for the Acid Splash.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The troll spins and goes running with Febron going (screaming) in its grasp, it starts lumbering, leaping from tree to tree. Its legs, while it has this bulbous, swollen midsection, these thin frog-like legs are giving these fairly decent leaps from section to section, its other arm grasping onto the trees as it runs. You can see this is definitely its home terrain. As these magic missiles swarm out and slam into its body, with each impact, that green ichor sprays across Febron, and it starts to burn into his skin. He's (screaming).

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is he unconscious? Is he done? Is that it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I rolled 2d8. I rolled eight on each of them. That was the reaction there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: Dexterity saving throw for the Acid Splash as well, if you don't mind.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. So you throw the ball bearings, and then everyone rolls initiative. You lost the surprise round, unfortunately. You were spending too much time talking.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: They look like murderous fish men.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're good to fuck them up, I think.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, I don't think that's a good idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I like how that sounds. (like Cali) Murderous fish men.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARK
preceding_turns.text: Have any of you seen Thor  Ragnarok?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! I was going to say, you sound like them, I love it!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm not half fish, I'm half-orc!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That is actually not true at all. Nott, not at all.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: You're half-orc, but what's the other half?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're glossing over it, but we've seen you vomit sea water twice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is this really the moment to talk about this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You know how under pressure, you can discover hidden talent? Maybe if you do this, you'll be like: Oh, I never knew this fishman side of me!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, jeez. We're losing our ambush, y'all.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Someone create a distraction! We'll pop out and kill it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What was the number you rolled?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As they're all eating, and Nott, you're paying attention, one goes, (inquisitive growl, garbled speech)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to Mage Hand throw the ball bearing first, just as a signal to go. On the three-two-one.
